title: EV sales are normalizing in the US – and TAKING OFF everywhere else
summary: On today’s totally normal episode of Quick Charge, it seems like EV sales are starting to normalize in the US after the loss of the Federal Tax Credit last September – but they’re taking off everywhere else, and Europe is leading the charge!

title: BYD’s 1,500+ hp EV supercar is cheaper and faster than a Porsche 911 Turbo S [Images]
summary: BYD launched the new Denza Z at the Goodwood FOS this weekend. While the electric supercar costs nearly $200,000, it’s still cheaper —and faster — than a Porsche 911 Turbo S.

title: Hyundai recalls a whopping 14 IONIQ 5 and Kia EVs, warning owners to park outside
summary: Hyundai issued a recall affecting a small number of older IONIQ 5, Kia EV9, and Kia EV6 models due to a battery defect that could lead to a fire.

title: Mercedes is now building its new electric C-Class – here’s where
summary: Mercedes-Benz has officially kicked off production of its upcoming all-electric C-Class at its factory in Kecskemét, Hungary, as the automaker expands EV production in Europe ahead of a wave of new electric models.

title: California’s new $3,500 EV rebate favors Rivian and Lucid over Tesla
summary:
California Governor Gavin Newsom signed SB 168 today, creating a new “MyFirstEV” program that gives first-time electric vehicle buyers a $3,500 instant rebate at the dealership starting later this summer.

The point-of-sale discount is backed by $135.5 million in state funding, matched dollar-for-dollar by participating automakers — and a California-headquarters rule makes Rivian and Lucid the biggest winners, while Tesla qualifies only on its cheapest models.

title: ENGWE 150-mile L16 Utility e-bike from $899, Segway Xyber e-bike + extra battery $1,400 off, Navimow i215 LiDAR robot, more
summary:
Kicking off this week’s Green Deals is the $300 savings on ENGWE’s newest L16 Utility e-bike (both single and dual battery models) that starts from $899. Right behind it we have the continuing $1,400 savings on Segway’s Xyber Electric Bike with a FREE extra battery at a $2,900 low, as well as the Navimow i215 LiDAR Robot Lawn Mower or a garage M bundle at their lowest prices starting from $1,399. We also have two Worx 1,870 or 2,090 max PSI compact pressure washer deals, some outdoor lighting, a comprehensive security system, and more waiting for you below. As always on a Monday, you can find last week’s hangover deals down at the bottom of the page, collected into our latest Electrified Weekly roundup edition.

Head below for other New Green Deals we’ve found today and, of course, Electrek’s best EV buying and leasing deals. Also, check out the new Electrek Tesla Shop for the best deals on Tesla accessories.

title: Tesla says it’s building a wheelchair-accessible robotaxi in Texas
summary:
Tesla is building a purpose-built, wheelchair-accessible autonomous vehicle, a company policy advisor told lawmakers in Washington, DC, on Monday.

But Tesla gave no timeline, no vehicle, and no details, and it’s not clear the “active product” is anything more than the Robovan it unveiled nearly two years ago.

title: The Lexus LFA electric supercar debuts with a fake V-8 roar [Video]
summary: Toyota’s luxury marque is bringing back its iconic supercar, but this time it’s all-electric. The Lexus LFA made its debut at the Goodwood FOS, revealing its low-slung design and fake V-8 roar.

title: BYD reveals new Qin Max EV with Flash Charging aimed at young buyers
summary: The Qin Max is a “completely different new B-segment fast-charging sedan,” BYD said after previewing the new EV for the first time.

title: Tesla Semi adds another fleet as Paper Transport starts Chicago pilot
summary:
Paper Transport (PTI) announced today that it is evaluating the Tesla Semi Long Range in dedicated operations in the Chicago market, becoming the latest fleet to put Tesla’s electric Class 8 truck to work in daily freight.

The Wisconsin-based carrier, which has logged more than 87 million miles on compressed and renewable natural gas, is adding battery-electric to a sustainability push it has been running for over 15 years.

title: California's $3,500 EV Rebate Program Starts 'Later This Summer'
summary: The program, which applies to new and used EVs, fills some of the gap left behind by the now-dead $7,500 EV tax credit.

title: Hyundai's Got An Awesome-Looking $30,000 EV. But It's Not For You
summary: With over 300 miles of WLTP range and charming styling, the Ioniq 3 would be an incredible Chevy Bolt competitor. But we're not that lucky.

title: Apple's Self-Driving Car Program Failed. But It Still Made Your iPhone Better
summary: Apple's self-driving car program failed. But the AI chipsets developed from it help power the iPhones and MacBooks on sale today.

title: After 87,000 Miles, This 2022 Tesla Model Y Didn’t Just Lose Range. It Charges Slower Too
summary: ADAC documented 14% battery-capacity loss and a substantial reduction in peak charging power after 87,000 miles.

title: This Chinese Micro EV Makes The Fiat Topolino Look Bare-Bones
summary: The €8,000 Astraux tiny EV has all the features you would expect to find in an actual car, despite technically not being one.

title: Toyota's EV Sales Have More Than Doubled This Year
summary: The company used to lag behind on EVs. Now it's growing its EV sales as others falter.

title: This YouTuber Put Extra Batteries In His F-150 EV's Bed To Boost Range
summary: A DIY Extended Range EV using power banks? It's more likely than you think.

title: The SkyNomad N90 Is Xiaomi’s Gas-Burning Adventure Rig
summary: The SkyNomad N90 is Xiaomi’s first EREV, and it’s a road-tripping family SUV built to haul people and gear.

title: The Rivian R2 Is Getting Launch Mode. It'll Be Quicker Than A Tesla Model Y Performance
summary: Rivian R2 is getting launch mode, making it faster in the quarter mile than its biggest competitor.

title: 'Public Trust Is Earned': Feds Are Officially Tired Of Robotaxis Getting In The Way Of First Responders
summary: One federal agency is telling robotaxi companies that it's time to stop interfering with police and firefighters.
